title: EV Powertrain Case Study
summary: Rules-compliant, reliable, quickly drivable; ~30% mass reduction via analysis-driven redesign.
text: Researched 40+ team designs; selected core components (Emrax motor + Drexler differential). Free-body diagrams for 5 major parts; ANSYS static structural + topology optimization. Vibration analysis to move natural frequency above motor operating range. ≈30% powertrain mass reduction vs prior iteration. Manufactured and tested for ~6 months without failures.

title: EV Accumulator (HV Battery)
summary: 440 cells, ~92 kW, 8.32 kWh; Nomex 410 + Kapton; custom 5-layer clad fuses (~95 A).
text: Defined design criteria with EE team; designed for strict FSAE rules and tight packaging. Thermal models showed passive cooling sufficient; eliminated active cooling complexity. Custom PCB for voltage/temperature logging; thermal relief features on busbars. Built and integrated in ~2 weeks. Ran reliably throughout the testing season.

title: Yamaha R6 Custom Motor Shaft
summary: Custom shaft to mate an R6 sprocket; JIS splines via MITCalc; 3D scan verification.
text: Designed spline geometry using Japanese automotive references in MITCalc. Snap-ring calculations, bearing fits, material/heat-treatment selection. Verified against 3D-scanned R6 model; assembled successfully.

title: Electronics Packaging
summary: Controller/electronics plate, HV/LV interface, VCU structure, cooling, steering jig, pedal mount.
text: Designed mounts and service access; ensured HV/LV isolation and strain relief. Used Fusion for plasma-cut programming; generative pedal mount exploration. Compact, serviceable layout; compliant with FSAE rules.

title: IWT Injection-Molding Ops
summary: In-sourcing + line optimization case study; alignment cones; updated shipping standard.
text: Supported in-sourcing of a high-volume stream (~400k units). Designed alignment cones; authored drawings and standards. Improved assembly efficiency and safety; validated with FEA checks.

title: Engineering Intern — Infiltrator Water Technologies
text: Engineering Intern Infiltrator Water Technologies Helped in-source and optimize an injection-molded product stream (~400k units). Designed alignment cones and authored manufacturing drawings/standards. Applied DFM for injection molding and ran FEA checks to validate parts.

title: EV Powertrain Lead — UConn Formula SAE
text: EV Powertrain Lead UConn Formula SAE Led EV powertrain development; integrated Drexler differential and Emrax motor. Built/packaged charging box, HV/LV box, shutdown box; ensured FSAE rule compliance. Structural analysis in ANSYS; iterative design reduced mass ~30% vs. prior iteration.

title: EV Powertrain Member — UConn Formula SAE
text: EV Powertrain Member UConn Formula SAE Contributed to design and integration of the team’s first EV powertrain. Assisted with battery, motor, and control-system integration; collaborated across subsystems.
